decision.primary_crux: The bounded inputs do not establish a stable contract: deadline/month wording conflicts across title, slug, metadata, and fetched contract text, so I cannot responsibly assess whether the market is pricing the same event the dispatch asks about.
secondary_cruxes: Even setting contract identity aside, the bundle says no independently confirmed official public-access artifact for V4 was found on checked first-party surfaces.
The market may still be incorporating real launch-imminence information, but the package explicitly says blockers require new research and the key first-party qualification evidence is missing.
A last-minute official open beta/open waitlist could flip the case immediately, which makes stale or mismatched contract wording especially dangerous.

valuation.compression_reason: No extra compression is useful because the main blocker is unresolved contract identity/deadline ambiguity, not overprecision around the provisional probability estimate.

portfolio_constraints: Do not trade from this packet because the operative deadline is not reliably identified across the bounded inputs.
Require confirmation of the actual governing contract text and deadline before authorizing any exposure.
Keep target exposure at zero until official first-party release/access evidence and contract wording are reconciled.

bands.name: max_enter
bands.notes: Zero-exposure band because the underlying contract is not cleanly identified.

bands.name: scaled_enter
bands.notes: Zero-exposure band because deadline and source-of-truth ambiguity block execution-quality judgment.

bands.name: hold
bands.notes: Zero-exposure band; no autonomous hold/add decision is justified until the contract text is reconciled.

bands.name: trim
bands.notes: Zero-exposure band because apparent mispricing may reflect contract mismatch rather than substantive disagreement.

bands.name: exit
bands.notes: Zero-exposure band; remain flat until deadline and qualification criteria are confirmed.

thesis_breakers: Authoritative confirmation shows the real contract deadline and qualification standard differ from what this packet assumes.
Official DeepSeek announces a clearly qualifying flagship successor with general-public accessibility before the true deadline.
Credible evidence establishes that no qualifying public-access release is possible by the true deadline.
market_structure_breakers: The dispatch, slug, and live market text are confirmed to refer to different contracts or deadlines.
A clarified rule interpretation changes what counts as public accessibility or flagship successor status.
Official DeepSeek surfaces materially update and resolve the current first-party evidence gap.
time_breakers: This packet should not be used after valid_until without contract clarification.
Because a single official launch/access announcement could move the case abruptly, stale pre-clarification judgments should not be carried forward.
reversal_conditions: No reversal logic applies because this packet authorizes no position.
A new packet is required after deadline reconciliation and refreshed official-source review.
Do not auto-reverse from flat based on this packet.

key_uncertainties: Whether the actual governing deadline is April 30 or some other date implied by conflicting market metadata.
Whether an official DeepSeek public-access artifact exists outside the checked first-party surfaces or could appear abruptly.
Whether rumor/reporting-based launch imminence is genuine enough to justify the market despite missing direct qualifying evidence.
reasons_to_pass: You cannot responsibly trade when the dispatch and fetched contract references disagree on the operative deadline/month.
The package does not independently verify an official public-access V4 release on first-party surfaces.
The runtime explicitly marks this case as reopen-recommended and says blockers require new research.
what_would_change_my_mind: Authoritative confirmation of the actual contract wording and deadline would make the case decision-ready enough to judge.
An official DeepSeek announcement plus clear general-public access path for the next flagship V-series model would move the probability sharply upward.
Credible confirmation that no qualifying official release/access artifact exists as the true deadline approaches would move it downward.

audit.one_sentence_rationale: Because the bounded inputs do not even cleanly identify the governing DeepSeek contract deadline and still lack first-party qualifying release evidence, the only disciplined output is to stay flat and demand clarification before trading.
audit.notes: I preserve the bounded provisional below-market estimate around 0.475 only as a placeholder expectation, not as an execution-quality signal; the real blocker is contract identity plus missing official qualifying evidence.
